mechanism_narrative: Persistent non-farm payroll growth signals ongoing labor market expansion which supports corporate earnings and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in greater than 50% of months with rising PAYEMS over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, elevated discount rates suppress near-term equity valuations and compress risk appetite, leading to negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 45% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of elevated consumer price inflation and a high nominal federal funds rate indicates strongly positive real policy rates that constrain aggregate demand and squeeze profit margins, generating negative 60-day S&P returns.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary regime with above-target inflation
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative during periods of joint high CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS above 4%, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.03 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is below 4%, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals that supply-side momentum is fading in a tight labor market, prompting equity risk repricing and negative 20-day S&P returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.0% and INDPRO declines month-over-month over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production momentum is a coincident indicator of corporate output; an acceleration in INDPRO over the prior three months predicts positive 60-day S&P returns via the earnings revision channel.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum does not coincide with positive 60-day S&P returns in greater than 55% of quarters over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand for construction, materials, and credit, forecasting negative 20-day S&P returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in greater than 55% of instances when HOUST falls more than 10% from its 6-month peak over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with sticky inflation indicates stagflationary pressure that erodes real earnings and forces tighter-for-longer monetary policy, producing negative 60-day S&P returns.
failure_condition: The UNRATE-CPIAUCSL interaction term exhibits Rank IC above -0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns are not negative in greater than 50% of high interaction observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth surprises capture aggregate demand shocks; above-trend GDP level relative to the prior year predicts positive 5-day S&P returns as the macro surprise is priced in rapidly.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following above-trend GDPC1 realizations are not positive in greater than 52% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
